questions.question: A $1,200\text{ kg}$ car rounds a flat curve of radius $80\text{ m}$. The coefficient of static friction between tires and road is $0.60$. What is the maximum speed the car can take the curve without skidding? ($g = 9.8\text{ m/s}^2$)
A car travels around a flat circular track at constant speed. What force keeps it from skidding outward?
options: The normal force from the road
Static friction between tires and road
questions.explanation: On a flat curve, static friction between the tires and the road provides the centripetal force directed toward the center.

questions.question: A car can safely round a flat curve at a maximum speed of $15\text{ m/s}$. If the radius of the curve is quadrupled (while the road surface stays the same), what is the new maximum safe speed?
questions.explanation: $v_{max} \propto \sqrt{r}$. Quadrupling $r$ means $v_{max}$ increases by $\sqrt{4} = 2$. New speed $= 15 \times 2 = 30\text{ m/s}$.

questions.question: A car rounds a flat curve of radius $40\text{ m}$ where the coefficient of static friction is $0.25$. What is the maximum safe speed? ($g = 10\text{ m/s}^2$)

A $0.20\text{ kg}$ ball is whirled in a horizontal circle of radius $0.60\text{ m}$ on a frictionless table, attached to a string. If the ball moves at $3.0\text{ m/s}$, what is the tension in the string?
The maximum speed a car can take a flat curve without skidding depends on:
options: The coefficient of friction and the radius of the curve
The car's mass and the radius
questions.explanation: $v_{max} = \sqrt{\mu_s g r}$. Mass cancels out — it depends only on friction coefficient and curve radius.

questions.question: A car can safely take a flat curve at a maximum speed of $14\text{ m/s}$. If $\mu_s = 0.50$ and $g = 9.8\text{ m/s}^2$, what is the radius of the curve?
In an amusement park rotor ride, riders stand against the wall of a spinning cylinder. The floor drops away but the riders remain pressed against the wall. What force keeps them from sliding down?
options: Static friction between the rider and the wall
questions.explanation: The wall pushes inward (normal force) providing $F_c$. Static friction upward balances the rider's weight.

questions.question: Curve A has radius $r$ and maximum safe speed $v$. Curve B has radius $4r$ with the same road surface. What is the maximum safe speed on Curve B?
A car safely rounds a curve of radius $64\text{ m}$ at a speed of $16\text{ m/s}$ on a flat road. What is the minimum coefficient of static friction required? ($g = 10\text{ m/s}^2$)
A ball on a string moves in a horizontal circle. If the speed is tripled while the radius remains the same, the tension in the string:
questions.explanation: $T = mv^2/r \propto v^2$. Tripling $v$ means $3^2 = 9$ times the tension.

questions.question: A $55\text{ kg}$ rider in a rotor ride spins in a circle of radius $3.0\text{ m}$ at a speed of $12\text{ m/s}$. What is the normal force the wall exerts on the rider?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered horizontal circular motion and its real-world applications!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the relationship $v_{max} = \sqrt{\mu_s g r}$ and how mass cancels out.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on identifying which force provides $F_c$ and using $F_c = mv^2/r$.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: centripetal force in horizontal circles, friction as $F_c$, and the maximum speed formula.
